questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Docker Compose?
options: To build Docker images faster
To define and run multi-container applications with a single YAML file
To replace the Docker daemon with a lightweight alternative
To manage Docker Hub repositories
questions.answer: To define and run multi-container applications with a single YAML file

questions.question: What is the default filename Docker Compose looks for when starting services?
options: compose.yaml
docker-compose.yml
services.yml
docker.yml
questions.answer: docker-compose.yml

questions.question: What command starts all services defined in a Compose file in the background?
options: docker compose start -d
docker compose run --detach
docker compose up -d
docker compose launch -d
questions.answer: docker compose up -d

questions.question: What does the 'docker compose down' command do?
options: Pauses all running containers
Stops containers, removes them, and removes the network
Only stops the containers without removing them
Deletes the docker-compose.yml file
questions.answer: Stops containers, removes them, and removes the network

questions.question: Which command lists the running services in a Compose project?
options: docker compose list
docker compose status
docker compose ps
docker compose services
questions.answer: docker compose ps

questions.question: How do containers in a Compose file communicate with each other by default?
options: By IP address only
Through shared volumes
By using the service name as the hostname
They cannot communicate without explicit network configuration
questions.answer: By using the service name as the hostname

questions.question: What is the difference between 'docker compose' (space) and 'docker-compose' (hyphen)?
options: They are completely different tools with different features
'docker compose' is the modern integrated plugin; 'docker-compose' is the older standalone version
'docker-compose' is the newer version
There is no difference; they are identical commands
questions.answer: 'docker compose' is the modern integrated plugin; 'docker-compose' is the older standalone version

questions.question: What does the 'ports' key do in a Compose service definition?
options: Exposes ports only to other containers in the Compose network
Maps host ports to container ports, making the service accessible from the host
Blocks incoming traffic on the specified ports
Opens ports on the Docker daemon
questions.answer: Maps host ports to container ports, making the service accessible from the host

questions.question: In the port mapping '8080:80', what does the left number represent?
options: The container port
The host port
The internal network port
The protocol number
questions.answer: The host port

questions.question: What does the 'depends_on' key do in a Compose file?
options: Shares environment variables between services
Links the filesystem of two containers
Controls the startup order of services
Ensures one service uses the same image as another
questions.answer: Controls the startup order of services

questions.question: What is the difference between 'ports' and 'expose' in a Compose file?
options: There is no difference; they are aliases
'ports' maps to the host; 'expose' only makes ports accessible to other Compose services
'expose' maps to the host; 'ports' is for internal use only
'ports' is for TCP and 'expose' is for UDP
questions.answer: 'ports' maps to the host; 'expose' only makes ports accessible to other Compose services

questions.question: How do you view logs from a specific service named 'api' in a Compose project?
options: docker compose logs --service api
docker compose logs api
docker logs compose-api
docker compose show-logs api
questions.answer: docker compose logs api

questions.question: What does the '-f' flag do when used with 'docker compose logs'?
options: Filters logs by severity level
Formats logs as JSON
Follows the log output in real time
Writes logs to a file
questions.answer: Follows the log output in real time

questions.question: How do you specify a named volume in a Compose file?
options: By listing it under a top-level 'volumes' key and referencing it in the service
By using the 'docker volume create' command before running Compose
By adding a VOLUME instruction to the Dockerfile only
Named volumes are not supported in Docker Compose
questions.answer: By listing it under a top-level 'volumes' key and referencing it in the service

questions.question: What does the 'build' key do in a Compose service definition?
options: Downloads a pre-built image from Docker Hub
Specifies the path to a Dockerfile to build a custom image for the service
Compiles the source code inside the container
Creates a backup of the service configuration
questions.answer: Specifies the path to a Dockerfile to build a custom image for the service

questions.question: What command rebuilds images defined with 'build' in a Compose file?
options: docker compose rebuild
docker compose build
docker compose up --rebuild
docker compose create
questions.answer: docker compose build

questions.question: How do you set environment variables for a service in a Compose file?
options: Using the 'env' key with key=value pairs
Using the 'environment' key with a list of KEY=VALUE entries
Using the 'variables' key with a mapping
Environment variables cannot be set in Compose files
questions.answer: Using the 'environment' key with a list of KEY=VALUE entries

questions.question: What does 'docker compose down -v' do that 'docker compose down' does not?
options: Removes the docker-compose.yml file
Also removes named volumes defined in the Compose file
Forces removal of running containers
Deletes the Docker images used by the services
questions.answer: Also removes named volumes defined in the Compose file

questions.question: Which command runs a one-off command inside a running Compose service?
options: docker compose run <service> <command>
docker compose exec <service> <command>
docker compose shell <service> <command>
docker compose attach <service> <command>
questions.answer: docker compose exec <service> <command>

questions.question: In the Compose file, what does 'image: nginx:alpine' specify?
options: A custom-built image named nginx with an alpine tag
The official Nginx image using the Alpine Linux variant from a registry
A local Dockerfile named nginx.alpine
An alias for the service container
questions.answer: The official Nginx image using the Alpine Linux variant from a registry

questions.question: What does the 'volumes' key under a service do when mapping './html:/usr/share/nginx/html'?
options: Creates a named volume called 'html'
Bind-mounts the local ./html directory into the container at the specified path
Copies the html directory into the container image permanently
Creates a symbolic link inside the container
questions.answer: Bind-mounts the local ./html directory into the container at the specified path

questions.question: What top-level keys are commonly found in a docker-compose.yml file?
options: containers, networks, storage
services, volumes, networks
images, ports, environment
build, deploy, run
questions.answer: services, volumes, networks

questions.question: In the Nginx reverse proxy configuration, what does 'proxy_pass http://api:5000' do?
options: Redirects the browser to port 5000 on the host machine
Forwards requests to the Compose service named 'api' on port 5000
Opens port 5000 on the Nginx container
Creates a new network route to port 5000
questions.answer: Forwards requests to the Compose service named 'api' on port 5000

questions.question: Why is a named volume used for the PostgreSQL data directory instead of a bind mount?
options: Named volumes are faster than bind mounts for all workloads
Bind mounts cannot store database files
Named volumes are managed by Docker and persist across container restarts without host path dependencies
Named volumes are the only option supported by PostgreSQL
questions.answer: Named volumes are managed by Docker and persist across container restarts without host path dependencies

questions.question: What happens to named volumes when you run 'docker compose down' without the '-v' flag?
options: They are deleted along with the containers
They are preserved and can be reused when services start again
They are moved to a backup directory
They become orphaned and must be manually reconnected
questions.answer: They are preserved and can be reused when services start again
